description: Initial analyzer-local P0 Rule Engine registry for deterministic vertical slice.

rules.definition: Selected scenario should expose a goal-relevant primary-like CTA in FIRST_VIEW or CTA stage.
rules.signal_rule: No primary-like CTA candidate is available in the stage context after deterministic signal extraction.

severity_rules.condition: primary-like CTA exists
only weak CTA candidates exist
no goal-relevant primary-like CTA in CTA stage
entrypoint disabled, blocked, or unreachable

rules.confidence_rule: High when DOM, AX, layout, and screenshot align; cap when semantic/provider confidence is low or visual-only evidence is present.
rules.output_template: 핵심 행동을 시작할 주요 행동 버튼이 충분히 드러나지 않아 다음 행동 선택이 어려울 수 있습니다.

references.label: USWDS Buttons
references.title: Button component
references.basisSummary: 중요한 행동은 사용자가 화면에서 바로 알아볼 수 있는 명확한 버튼이나 링크로 제공되어야 합니다.

references.label: NN/g Heuristics
references.title: Recognition rather than recall
references.basisSummary: 사용자가 다음 행동을 기억해내기보다 화면에서 인식할 수 있도록 명확한 선택지를 보여주는 것이 좋습니다.

rules.definition: Primary-like CTAs should not excessively compete inside the same decision context.
rules.signal_rule: primary_like_cta_count >= 3 in the CTA decision context.

severity_rules.condition: primary-like CTA count is 0-2
primary-like CTA count is 3 or more

rules.confidence_rule: Use cluster observation confidence when present; otherwise use aggregate-signal medium confidence.
rules.output_template: 같은 결정 순간에서 여러 강조된 행동 버튼이 경쟁해 사용자가 첫 행동을 고르기 어려울 수 있습니다.

references.label: USWDS Button Group
references.title: Button group component
references.basisSummary: 서로 관련된 버튼은 함께 배치하되, 사용자가 각 행동의 차이와 우선순위를 쉽게 이해할 수 있어야 합니다.

rules.definition: Multi-step flows should provide an explicit way to go back, edit, or change a prior step.
rules.signal_rule: A multi-step flow is detected but no visible back/edit/change affordance is present.

severity_rules.condition: explicit back, previous, edit, or change affordance is visible
only browser history back appears available
no explicit back affordance and browser history back is unavailable or unknown

exceptions: Suppress when the page is not confirmed to be part of a multi-step flow.
Suppress when a visible previous/edit/change/cancel affordance is present.
rules.confidence_rule: Use path_navigation confidence; cap confidence when only browser history back is available.
rules.output_template: 다단계 흐름에서 이전 단계로 돌아가거나 내용을 수정하는 방법이 명확하지 않습니다.

references.label: GOV.UK Back link
references.title: Back link component
references.basisSummary: 여러 단계로 이어지는 서비스에서는 흐름에 맞게 이전 단계로 돌아갈 수 있는 명확한 방법을 제공하는 것이 좋습니다.

rules.definition: Primary-like CTA controls should not be hidden inside collapsed accordion panels during decision stages.

severity_rules.condition: no collapsed accordion hides a primary-like CTA
a collapsed accordion hides a primary-like CTA in VALUE or CTA stage

exceptions: Suppress when the accordion is expanded.
Suppress when the hidden panel contains only secondary links, required information, or non-primary actions.
rules.confidence_rule: Use accordion_state confidence; primary-like CTA detection is supplied by Runner DOM/layout heuristics.
rules.output_template: 중요한 행동 버튼이 접힌 버튼 안에 숨겨져 있습니다.

references.label: GOV.UK Accordion
references.title: Accordion component
references.basisSummary: 접힌 부분 밖에 사용자가 원하는 정보와 행동을 발견할 수 있도록 구성해야 합니다.

rules.definition: A choice area should not pack many clickable choices so tightly that users struggle to distinguish or select them.
rules.signal_rule: Group visible, enabled, clickable components by choice area and evaluate density using component bounds: issue only when the group has 7 or more choices, median adjacent gap is 8px or less, and occupied axis ratio is at least 0.75.

severity_rules.condition: choice group has fewer than 7 choices, median adjacent gap is over 8px, or occupied axis ratio is below 0.75
choice group has 7 or more choices with median adjacent gap of 8px or less and occupied axis ratio of at least 0.75
choice group has 10 or more choices with median adjacent gap of 6px or less, tight pair ratio of at least 0.5, and occupied axis ratio of at least 0.8
choice group has 15 or more choices with median adjacent gap of 4px or less, tight pair ratio of at least 0.65, and occupied axis ratio of at least 0.85 in a high-impact action stage

rules.confidence_rule: Use observation confidence and cap confidence when viewport dimensions are missing.
rules.output_template: 한 화면에 클릭 가능한 선택지가 너무 많이 동시에 노출됩니다.

references.label: GOV.UK Components
references.title: Components
references.basisSummary: 같은 결정 영역 안에 클릭 가능한 선택지가 너무 많거나 촘촘하게 배치되면, 사용자가 다음 행동을 고르기 어려워질 수 있습니다.

rules.definition: Visible input fields should provide a label, accessible name, or equivalent instruction.
rules.signal_rule: A visible form field has neither clear label/instruction nor accessible name when label relation evidence is available.

severity_rules.condition: visible label or accessible name is clear
placeholder-only label support
visible field has no label or accessible name

rules.confidence_rule: 입력 필드에 목적을 알 수 있는 라벨이나 설명이 있는지 판단한다.
rules.output_template: 입력 필드의 목적을 알려주는 라벨이나 안내가 부족해 입력 진행이 어려울 수 있습니다.

references.label: WCAG 3.3.2
references.title: Labels or Instructions
references.basisSummary: 입력을 요구하는 화면에서는 사용자가 무엇을 입력해야 하는지 알 수 있는 라벨 또는 안내가 제공되어야 합니다.

rules.definition: Required fields should be communicated before submit, not only after a required-field validation error.
rules.signal_rule: A visible form_field has submit_required_error=true but no visible required marker, required text, or group-level required indication.

severity_rules.condition: required status is visible before submit, or no submit-time required error occurs
a required-field error appears after submit without a prior required marker
a required-field error appears without a prior required marker in a payment, order, or other sensitive flow

exceptions: Suppress hidden, disabled, submit, button, reset, and non-user-entered fields.
Do not emit from DOM required alone; submit_required_error must be observed.
rules.confidence_rule: High when submit_required_error is paired with DOM/AX required state; cap confidence when only the submit error signal is available.
rules.output_template: 필수 입력 여부가 제출 후 오류를 통해서만 전달됩니다.

references.label: WCAG 3.3.2
references.title: Labels or Instructions
references.basisSummary: 폼을 제출하기 전에 사용자가 어떤 입력이 필요한지 알 수 있도록 라벨이나 안내를 제공해야 합니다.

references.label: GOV.UK Error Message
references.title: Error message component
references.basisSummary: 폼 오류는 무엇이 잘못됐고 어떻게 고칠 수 있는지 알려줘야 하며, 필수 입력 기대값도 명확해야 합니다.

rules.definition: Visible labels should match the UI element role, function, or surrounding context.
rules.signal_rule: A GMS label-role alignment result marks a candidate as status=mismatch with sufficient confidence.

severity_rules.condition: label_role_alignment is aligned or unavailable
GMS reports a low-impact label-role mismatch
GMS reports a mismatch on a primary, clicked, or high-leverage element
GMS reports a high-leverage mismatch on a commit-stage action

rules.confidence_rule: Use sanitized GMS label-role confidence only; deterministic observation type or direct label_issue_type fields do not emit this rule.
rules.output_template: 화면에 보이는 라벨이 요소의 역할이나 예상되는 다음 행동과 맞지 않습니다.

references.label: WCAG 2.4.6
references.title: Headings and Labels
references.basisSummary: 버튼이나 라벨 문구는 주변 화면 흐름과 어울리고, 사용자가 기대하는 행동을 헷갈리지 않게 해야 합니다.

rules.definition: Visible labels and short copy should be readable without broken, clipped, overlapped, or truncated rendering.
rules.signal_rule: A deterministic or GMS label_integrity result marks a candidate as status=issue with an allowed integrity issue type.

severity_rules.condition: label_integrity is ok or unavailable
low-impact readability issue
broken, clipped, overlapped, or path-related label issue
commit-stage high-leverage integrity issue

rules.confidence_rule: Use sanitized label_integrity confidence when present; deterministic text corruption signals may also emit.
rules.output_template: 화면에 보이는 라벨이나 짧은 문구를 읽기 어렵습니다.

references.label: WCAG 1.4.12
references.title: Text Spacing
references.basisSummary: 텍스트 간격이나 레이아웃 변화가 문구의 잘림, 겹침, 정보 손실을 만들지 않아야 합니다.

rules.definition: Meaningful user actions should produce a visible result confirmation when no technical failure explains the missing outcome.
rules.signal_rule: A goal-like action is attempted, no visible success/change evidence is present, and no same-stage network or console failure suppresses the feedback issue.

severity_rules.condition: visible result confirmation exists, such as toast, URL change, DOM change, or count increase
only a network success signal exists after a non-commit action
no visible result confirmation after CTA or INPUT action, or only network success after COMMIT action
no visible result confirmation after COMMIT action, or settle_status is timeout/failed on COMMIT

exceptions: Suppress when the action is not goal-like or meaningful.
Suppress when network or console failures are present, because RELIABILITY-TECH-001 should explain the primary technical failure.
Suppress when visible result evidence is present in goal_action_result or same-checkpoint settle_item_count_change.
rules.confidence_rule: Use goal_action_result confidence; cap when only network_success exists because that is not directly user-visible feedback.
rules.output_template: 사용자 행동 이후 결과가 화면에서 명확히 확인되지 않습니다.

references.label: NN/g Heuristics
references.title: Visibility of System Status
references.basisSummary: 시스템이 사용자의 행동을 받았고 처리했는지 사용자가 적절한 시간 안에 볼 수 있어야 합니다.

rules.definition: Technical failures should be surfaced only when they block or plausibly break the next result of a user action or a core page resource.
rules.signal_rule: User-impacting failed requests or console errors are present in stage-attributed action context evidence.

severity_rules.condition: no failed request or console error evidence
one or more failed requests or console errors after action

rules.confidence_rule: High for direct network/console observations or checkpoint-local state summaries. Run-level aggregate-only counters are diagnostic and do not emit staged issues.
rules.output_template: 사용자 행동 직후 기술 오류가 관찰되어 진행 신뢰성이 낮아질 수 있습니다.

references.label: NN/g Heuristics
references.title: Visibility of System Status
references.basisSummary: 사용자는 자신의 행동 이후 시스템 상태와 오류 여부를 적절한 시간 안에 알 수 있어야 합니다.

rules.definition: Long-running user actions should expose a visible processing state when no technical failure explains the delay.
rules.signal_rule: A loading_state or settle_response lasts at least 3000ms, no visible processing feedback is present, and same-stage technical failures are absent.

severity_rules.condition: duration is under 3000ms, or spinner/progress/status text/aria-busy feedback is present
duration is 3000-4999ms and no visible processing feedback is present
duration is 5000ms or more, or an important submit/save/checkout action lacks processing feedback
COMMIT-stage timeout or 8000ms or more with no processing feedback

exceptions: Suppress when network or console failures are present, because RELIABILITY-TECH-001 should explain the primary technical failure.
Suppress when duration is below 3000ms.
Suppress when spinner, progressbar, status text, aria-busy, or an equivalent visible loading message is present.
rules.confidence_rule: Use loading_state confidence when available; cap settle_response-only cases because absence of visible status is inferred.
rules.output_template: 사용자가 기다리는 동안 처리 중 상태가 화면에 명확히 보이지 않습니다.

references.label: NN/g Heuristics
references.title: Visibility of System Status
references.basisSummary: 사용자는 특히 행동이 처리되는 동안 시스템이 무엇을 하고 있는지 적절한 시간 안에 피드백을 받아야 합니다.

rules.definition: A normal navigation action should render the next page or result view into a usable state within the expected ready threshold.
rules.signal_rule: A general navigation action has duration_ms >= 5000 when all configured target_page_signals are false.

severity_rules.condition: normal navigation becomes usable under 5000ms, or one configured target_page_signals flag is true
normal navigation page ready duration is 5000ms or more
normal navigation page ready duration is 8000ms or more, or a commit-stage navigation is delayed

exceptions: Suppress when the action is not a normal navigation/link/menu/tab transition.
Suppress when target_page_signals has has_permission_prompt, has_streaming_response, has_map, has_payment_form, has_auth_redirect, or has_webgl set to true.
Suppress when network or console failures are present, because RELIABILITY-TECH-001 should explain the primary technical failure.
rules.confidence_rule: High when direct page_ready_timing evidence exists; cap confidence when using loading_state or settle_response fallback only.
rules.output_template: 일반적인 이동 행동 이후 다음 화면이나 결과 화면이 사용 가능한 상태가 되기까지 오래 걸립니다.

references.label: NN/g Heuristics
references.title: Visibility of System Status
references.basisSummary: 지연이 발생하는 동안 사용자가 시스템 상태를 이해할 수 있어야 하며, 일반 전환이 오래 걸리면 진행 신뢰가 낮아질 수 있습니다.

rules.definition: A selected CTA should be semantically aligned with the scenario goal.
rules.signal_rule: A clicked CTA receives a low scenario relevance semantic label with sufficient provider confidence.

severity_rules.condition: CTA is directly or reasonably related to the scenario goal
CTA is auxiliary or weakly related to the scenario goal
CTA is irrelevant to the scenario goal

rules.confidence_rule: Use sanitized semantic provider confidence and do not emit an issue below the semantic confidence threshold.
rules.output_template: 선택된 행동 버튼이 시나리오 목표와 약하게 연결되어 보입니다.

references.label: NN/g Heuristics
references.title: Match between system and the real world
references.basisSummary: 사용자가 선택한 행동의 문구와 결과는 사용자의 목표와 기대하는 실제 작업 흐름에 맞아야 합니다.

references.label: Baymard Checkout
references.title: Checkout usability research
references.basisSummary: 목표 흐름의 단계와 행동 문구가 맞지 않으면 사용자가 다음 행동을 오해하거나 이탈할 수 있습니다.

rules.definition: Checkout, booking, order, payment, or application flows should show a review summary before the final submit action.
rules.signal_rule: A final checkout submit action is detected but checkout_context.has_order_summary is false.

severity_rules.condition: a review summary is present before or near the final submit action
a non-payment final checkout, order, booking, or application submit action has no review summary
a payment final submit action has no review summary

exceptions: Suppress when no final submit action is present.
Do not require an edit/change affordance for this rule; only review-summary presence is evaluated.
rules.confidence_rule: Use checkout_context confidence and cap at 0.86 because checkout classification is heuristic.
rules.output_template: 사용자가 주문 또는 예약 정보를 검토하기 전에 최종 제출 행동이 먼저 나타납니다.

references.label: WCAG 3.3.4
references.title: Error Prevention (Legal, Financial, Data)
references.basisSummary: 중요한 제출에서는 사용자가 최종 확정 전에 정보를 검토, 확인, 수정할 기회를 가져야 합니다.

rules.definition: Scenario safety blocks should be surfaced as a journey boundary rather than a generic analysis failure.
rules.signal_rule: A runner_failure observation has a POLICY_*_BLOCKED failure_code in the stage context.

severity_rules.condition: no scenario safety block is observed
external, synthetic input, or other recoverable/non-commit safety boundary is observed
payment commit, destructive action, or COMMIT-stage safety boundary is observed

exceptions: Do not emit from aggregate counters alone; the issue must reference a runner_failure observation.
Do not classify the block as a technical page failure unless separate network or console evidence exists.
rules.confidence_rule: High when Runner supplies a stage-attributed runner_failure observation with a POLICY_*_BLOCKED failure_code.
rules.output_template: 선택한 흐름이 안전 정책 경계에 닿아 자동 실행이 안전하게 멈췄습니다.

references.label: NN/g Heuristics
references.title: Visibility of system status
references.basisSummary: 사용자는 현재 흐름이 멈춘 이유와 다음에 무엇을 해야 하는지 이해할 수 있어야 합니다.

rules.definition: Product cards should not appear without visible product imagery when image resource failures are observed in the same checkpoint.
rules.signal_rule: A product_card has visible_product_image=false with product evidence such as price, and the same checkpoint has failed image network evidence.

severity_rules.condition: product cards have visible images, or no image resource failure is observed
one to three product cards have no visible image with same-checkpoint image resource failure
four or more product cards have no visible image with same-checkpoint image resource failure

exceptions: Suppress when no same-checkpoint image network failure is observed.
Suppress cards without price or equivalent product evidence.
rules.confidence_rule: Use product_card layout confidence and raise confidence only when image network failure evidence is present in the same checkpoint.
rules.output_template: 상품 카드에서 상품 이미지가 보이지 않아 상품 확인과 비교가 어려울 수 있습니다.

rules.definition: Goal-relevant interactive targets should be large enough and spaced enough for accurate pointer or touch activation.
rules.signal_rule: Visible enabled interactive buttons, links, and icon controls are evaluated only when they were used in the scenario, are CTA candidates, are required choice controls, or match core action keywords; text-entry fields such as input, textarea, textbox, searchbox, and combobox are suppressed; goal-relevant targets with bounds smaller than 24 CSS px, or below 44 CSS px with tight neighboring target spacing, are flagged.

severity_rules.condition: all countable targets are at least 24 CSS px, or smaller targets have sufficient spacing/exception context
a target is smaller than 24 CSS px but not tightly spaced, or a 24-44 CSS px target is tightly spaced
a target is smaller than 24 CSS px and spacing is tight or unknown
a target is smaller than 16 CSS px and spacing is tight or unknown

exceptions: Suppress hidden, disabled, aria-hidden, or off-viewport targets.
Suppress header/footer utility and legal/help links.
Suppress text-entry fields such as search inputs even when they are clickable or form controls.
Suppress targets that are merely clickable but not scenario-used, CTA-like, required choice controls, or matched to core action keywords.
Treat 44 CSS px as an enhanced usability target, not a hard failure threshold.
rules.confidence_rule: Use interactive_components confidence and cap confidence when viewport dimensions are missing.
rules.output_template: 일부 클릭 대상이 너무 작거나 서로 가까워 정확히 누르기 어렵습니다.

references.label: Lighthouse
references.title: Tap targets are not sized appropriately
references.basisSummary: 클릭 대상이 작으면 사용자가 원하는 대상을 정확히 선택하기 어려울 수 있습니다.
